description_long: This mud patch features an uneven, rugged surface with a mix of compacted earth and loose, crumbly soil. The coloration ranges from light tan to darker brown, with some areas appearing slightly moist and others dry. The patch has irregular, jagged edges and a somewhat sloped profile, suggesting it may have formed from runoff or heavy foot traffic. There are no visible impressions or marks, and the surface texture is coarse, with small clumps and granular details. The mud appears dense and solid, likely able to support moderate weight without significant deformation. No debris, water accumulation, or foreign objects are present on or around the patch.
description: An irregular, coarse mud patch with a mix of light and dark brown tones, featuring a rugged, sloped surface and jagged edges.
description_view_0: From this angle, the mud patch shows a prominent slope and a darker, more compacted section on the left side.
description_view_1: This view highlights the lighter, drier surface and the irregular, slightly elevated edge at the back.
description_view_2: Here, the patch appears more elongated, with a visible dip and a rough, uneven texture across the surface.
description_view_3: The front-facing view reveals the patch's broad, flat area and the granular, clumpy soil texture.
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, with no sharp transitions.
